name: VLM/MMLM · Tradeoff-Grounded Variant
full_intro_template: P1 Context: <VLM task> requires aligning visual evidence and language reasoning in <deployment setting>.
P2 Gap: Current systems suffer from hallucination, weak grounding, or brittle long-context reasoning.
P3 Failure diagnosis: Prior approaches overfit shortcut correlations and underuse fine-grained visual cues.
P4 Reframing: We separate grounding, reasoning, and calibration into coordinated modules.
P5 Method intuition: <grounding module> links phrases to regions; <reasoning module> composes evidence chains.
P6 Evaluation contract: Report quality metrics and hallucination/faithfulness metrics jointly.
P7 Cost contract: Report token budget, latency, and memory overhead under matched settings.
P8 Limitation boundary: Analyze failure under long-tail concepts, cluttered scenes, and ambiguous language.
P9 Contributions: (i) grounded design, (ii) controllable inference, (iii) comprehensive evaluation.
abstract_template: A1: Define task and deployment setting with one measurable constraint.
A2: State precise gap and why prior work fails in that regime.
A3: Present method/protocol and strongest baseline delta under matched budget.
A4: Add uncertainty (mean±std/significance) and one limitation boundary.
method_template: M1 Define task formulation and evaluation protocol.
M2 Describe visual encoder and language interface.
M3 Detail grounding module and evidence extraction.
M4 Detail reasoning module and calibration mechanism.
M5 Explain training objectives, data mixture, and regularization.
M6 Provide inference complexity and token-cost analysis.
experiments_template: E1 Benchmark suite and protocol disclosure with matched budgets.
E2 Main results on accuracy/grounding/faithfulness metrics.
E3 Hallucination and stability stress tests.
E4 Ablation on grounding and reasoning modules.
E5 Error taxonomy with representative qualitative cases.
E6 Cost-quality frontier across model scales or token budgets.
falsifiable_claims: If <core module> is removed, performance on <hard subset> should decrease by >= <delta>.
Under matched training/inference budget, <our method> should outperform <strong baseline> on <metric>.
delta_vs_strongest_baseline: Compared with <strong baseline>, we add <mechanism> and obtain <delta> under matched budget and protocol.
limitations_or_failure_cases: We explicitly analyze failure cases under <scenario> and report why the method is not dominant there.
contribution_schema: Contribution: <type> | Evidence: <verifiable artifact> | Risk/Boundary: <where it may fail>

operational_definition.B: resource or reliability metric reported jointly

paragraph_cards.template: P1 Context: strong capability but unstable grounding.
paragraph_cards.evidence_hook: Link this paragraph to a concrete artifact: Table/Figure/Theorem/Ablation.

anti_pattern_examples.bad: We achieve SOTA on all benchmarks.
anti_pattern_examples.better: Under matched training/inference budgets, we outperform strong baselines on <metric> over <subset>.

anti_pattern_examples.bad: This is the first work to study <task>.
anti_pattern_examples.better: To the best of our knowledge, this is one of the first unified treatments under <assumptions>.

anti_pattern_examples.bad: Our method is reliable.
anti_pattern_examples.better: We quantify stability on <stress test> and report failure cases on <hard subset>.

statistical_reliability_requirements: Report mean±std over >=3 random seeds when stochastic training is used.
State whether significance testing was performed and which test was used.
cost_disclosure_requirements: Report at least one cost metric from: token cost, latency, GPU memory.
Compare cost under matched hardware/batch settings.

reorder_profiles.description: context -> gap -> idea -> evidence -> limitation -> contributions
context -> evidence -> gap -> idea -> limitation -> contributions
context -> protocol -> gap -> idea -> evidence -> contributions

conclusion_template: C2 Highlight reliable gains and deployment cost trade-offs.
C3 Discuss ethical/stability limits and future work.
